$comment: Global economy constants from design doc §8. All first-pass values — tune in Phase 3 against pacing targets.

gifts.$note: cost(n) = base * growth^n where n = familiars of that role already befriended this run. Gatherer gifts are paid in the node's own resource (design §13 decision: you leave a pile of what the flock likes), gathererBaseGoods units from camp stock. Carrier gifts fill the camp Feeder: carrierBaseGoods units of EACH resource the flock currently works (every node with a gatherer), also from camp stock — breadth for the familiars that serve every trail. First-pass values, tune in Phase 3.

hauling.$note: first-guess Phase-1 numbers, not in design doc — a carrier's throughput is baseCarryCapacity/tripSeconds (x carry-capacity upgrades); gathered goods wait in a per-node basket of basketCapacity and overflow is lost. Tune with the loop playtest.

familiarCaps.$note: design §8: flockCap(zone) = base + perLevel·roostLevel (gatherers per zone) · carrierSlots = base + perLevel·roostLevel (camp-wide; the +1 Spare Wing reward arrives with the PGS layer). Roost level = bought levels of the roosts building line.

crafting.$note: first-guess Phase-3 number, not in design doc — seconds per craft at every station before craftSpeedMult upgrades divide it. Tune against the §2 pacing targets (first recipe cooked ~20 min).

tools.$note: flint is knapped (no forge); each later tier also consumes an ingot batch (see recipes)

mastery.$note: curve and xpPerUnit are first guesses, not in the design doc — mastery XP per unit gathered at the node, rung r costs base·growth^r. yieldBonusPerLevel applies to both yield and the raw resource's sell value (§4 '+5% yield/value').

xp.$note: gatherPerUnit/craftPerBatch are first guesses, not in the design doc — XP per unit gathered (gross, basket overflow included) and per completed batch. Tune with the §2 pacing targets.

quality.$note: design §5: quality rolls per haul batch (Common 96% / Fine ~3.5% +50% value / Pristine 0.5% base). pristineValueMult is a first guess — the 'windfall' sale isn't priced in the doc. pristineChance = (pristineBaseChance + owned pristineChanceBonus effects) * (1 + tending.pristineChanceBonus while the node's tend window is live) — flat bonuses add points, Tending multiplies (§8).

amber.$note: Amber (design §10): the premium currency's free earn + soft sink. Dig finds roll per tick at diggers * digFindsPerHour * digSpeedMult (a separate channel from fragments — fully-dug ground keeps surfacing amber); a time-skip instantly credits timeSkipHours of full live production for timeSkipCostAmber. All first guesses against the ~40/week-free lean; IAP/ads/weekly cache arrive with the plugin pass.

excavation.$note: drop rate = digFamiliars * baseFragmentsPerHour * digSpeedMult upgrades * the site's summed incomplete strataRarity, rolled per tick; a fragment is guaranteed once pityTimerHoursDug hours of digging pass without one. baseFragmentsPerHour is a first guess — tune so the first fossil lands inside the MVP playtest.

tending.$note: burst values are first guesses — tune in Phase 1.

warden.$note: first guess. The warden's own hands at their post (the last-tended node) — always on, boosted by a live burst, straight to camp stock. It's the early-game kickstart (assignment, not a tap surge) AND how a bare node earns its first own-resource replant (design §3), so it must stay positive.

familiarXp.$note: design §4: familiar run XP + Kinship. famXPToLevel(L) = base·growth^L (60·1.12^L), a powerup choice every 5 levels. xpPerSecond = base run XP a stationed familiar earns per second at its post; a wanderer earns half (§2). At Migration run XP converts to Kinship: kinshipGain = floor(√(runXP / kinshipDivisor)); each Kinship level gives +kinshipXpRatePerLevel XP rate and a higher starting level. Levels never scale output. All first guesses; postMatch/comfort multipliers (§8) are a later refinement (todo.md).
